questions.q: Define Faraday's first law of electrolysis.
questions.answer: Faraday's first law of electrolysis states that the mass of a substance deposited at an electrode during electrolysis is proportional to the quantity of electricity passing through the electrolyte.
key_points: Mass of substance is directly proportional to the quantity of electricity

questions.q: State the relationship between the potential of a cell and the standard electrode potential.
questions.answer: The potential of a cell (E_cell) is given by E_cell = E^°(cathode) - E^°(anode), where E^°(cathode) and E^°(anode) are the standard electrode potentials of the cathode and anode, respectively.

questions.q: What is the significance of Nernst equation?
questions.answer: The Nernst equation relates the electrode potential of a cell to the standard electrode potential and the concentrations of the ions involved.
key_points: Relate electrode potential to standard electrode potential and concentrations

questions.answer: A concentration cell is an electrochemical cell in which the potential difference arises due to a difference in concentration of the ions on the two electrodes.
key_points: Potential difference due to concentration difference

questions.q: What is the significance of the term 'electrolytic conductivity'?
questions.answer: Electrolytic conductivity is the ability of an electrolyte to conduct electricity when an electric potential is applied across it.
key_points: Ability to conduct electricity in an electrolyte

questions.q: Describe the factors affecting the conductivity of an electrolyte.
questions.answer: The conductivity of an electrolyte is affected by the concentration of the electrolyte, temperature, and the nature of the electrolyte.

questions.q: Explain the working of a fuel cell.
questions.answer: A fuel cell is an electrochemical cell that converts chemical energy into electrical energy by reacting a fuel with an oxidizing agent. The reaction occurs at the anode and cathode, and the electrical energy is produced at the external circuit.
Corrosion is an electrochemical process that involves the oxidation of a metal in the presence of oxygen and moisture, leading to the formation of oxides and other compounds.
There are three main types of corrosion: galvanic corrosion, crevice corrosion, and pitting corrosion.

questions.q: Describe the process of electrolysis of water.
questions.answer: The electrolysis of water involves the decomposition of water into hydrogen and oxygen gas, using an electric current. The reaction occurs at the anode and cathode, and the products are collected at the respective electrodes.

questions.q: Explain the construction and working of a lead storage battery.
questions.answer: A lead storage battery consists of lead plates and sulfuric acid as the electrolyte. The battery works by the reaction between the lead plates and the sulfuric acid, which produces electricity.
key_points: Reaction between lead and sulfuric acid

questions.q: Describe the process of electrolysis of molten sodium chloride.
questions.answer: The electrolysis of molten sodium chloride involves the decomposition of sodium chloride into sodium and chlorine gas, using an electric current. The reaction occurs at the anode and cathode, and the products are collected at the respective electrodes.

Electroplating involves the deposition of a thin layer of a metal onto the surface of another metal using an electric current. The process involves the oxidation of the metal ions in the electrolyte and their reduction at the cathode.
key_points: Oxidation and reduction of metal ions

questions.answer: Electrorefining involves the removal of impurities from a metal by electrolysis. The process involves the oxidation of the impurities at the anode and their collection at the cathode.

questions.q: Explain the principles of the chlor-alkali process.
questions.answer: The chlor-alkali process involves the electrolysis of sodium chloride to produce chlorine gas and sodium hydroxide. The process involves the oxidation of chlorine at the anode and the reduction of water at the cathode.
